column7: Typo ‚Äď Please note that this correction is confirmed by VCP, SKD, SHS and MW. This appears to be a common problem with AP. column6: Namaste Dr. Dhaval, I would like you to review the following placement of the Headword: "havyńĀ f. N. of a cow, DrńĀhy. [ID=262048.3 sup]". Being a Supplemental Entry, the location with the Main Entries needs to be decided. This issue has previously been discussed, but I have discovered new evidence that requires a reappraisal.  Currently, the placement of "havyńĀ" is under the Headword "2. havya". Andhrabharati prefers this placements because a Name (which havyńĀ is) is a thing "to be called", which is the meaning of "2. havya". However, please consider first the following point: Any Proper Name has Two Qualities: 1) The function served in "Calling" the thing so Named. Of course, this function is common and shared by All Names;  2) The particular meaning inherent in the Name itself, which in my view would be More Important in deciding the Etymological Placement. We should not prioritize the Etymological Placement of a Name based upon the Meaning of "To Call", because ALL Names serve this primary function!  Now, please consider the following New Evidence that I have found in support of the Relocation of the Headword: "havyńĀ f. N. of a cow", so as to be placed under the Headword "1. havya". This Evidence is found in SHS Dictionary under the Headword: "bńĀla [ID=28682]". In particular, please reference the following Meaning given therein for the Feminine Form: "bńĀlńĀ" in the final Definition Listed: "9. Pure, (applied specially to a female animal, as a heifer, &c. fit for an oblation.)". Now, please notice the Emphatic Word Used Here: "specially"; this means NOT a Casual or Marginal Point! It means "Especially Applied To a Female Animal as a Heifer (a young Female COW that has not yet borne a calf) FIT FOR AN OBLATION!". What is a Name? It is a Word "Especially Applied" to a thing (for a particular reason, i.e. not randomly). Now, Please consider the Meaning of "1. havya" ‚Äď "anything to be offered as an oblation". Obviously there is a correlation here between three points: 1) The SHS Definition Cited above, 2) the Headword "1. havya" and 3) the Supplemental Entry In MW Dictionary: "havyńĀ f. N. of a cow, DrńĀhy. [ID=262048.3 sup]". So the Question For YOUR Evaluation is this: WHAT is this proper Etymological Placement for the Supplementary Headword "havyńĀ", based upon all considerations presented here.
